content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN containing **distinct** integers and an integer KKK (either 000 or 111).

Your task is to find the total number of permutations of array AAA such that for **all** pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j)
with 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N, and (i+j)(i + j)(i+j) being an **odd** number:


(Ai+Aj)%2(A_i+A_j) \% 2(Ai​+Aj​)%2 =K= K=K
You should output the count of such permutations modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK, as mentioned in statement.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the total number of permutations of array AAA satisfying the conditions, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: There is no permutation that satisfies the required conditions.
Test Case 222: All the permutations of the array satisfy the required conditions.
Test Case 333: Two permutations satisfy the conditions. They are:

[1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3]: The pairs under consideration are (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) and (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3). Here (A1+A2)%2=1=K(A_1+A_2) \% 2 = 1 = K(A1​+A2​)%2=1=K. Similarly (A2+A3)%2=1=K(A_2+A_3) \% 2 = 1 = K(A2​+A3​)%2=1=K.
[3,2,1][3,2,1][3,2,1] The pairs under consideration are (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) and (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3). Here (A1+A2)%2=1=K(A_1+A_2) \% 2 = 1 = K(A1​+A2​)%2=1=K. Similarly (A2+A3)%2=1=K(A_2+A_3) \% 2 = 1 = K(A2​+A3​)%2=1=K.
